# Publishing Your Module {#sec-publishing}

This chapter covers how to submit a new community module, update an existing one, and what quality criteria to meet for both community and official JASP modules. The submission process uses Git and GitHub — if you're not yet comfortable with those, review the [Git basics](setup.qmd#sec-git-setup) in the setup chapter and the [Git Workflow](git-workflow.qmd) chapter first.

## Community Module Submission

Community modules are managed through the [`modules-registry`](https://github.com/jasp-stats-modules/modules-registry) repository on GitHub under the [jasp-stats-modules](https://github.com/jasp-stats-modules) organization. To add your module, choose one of the two options below.

### Option 1: Contact the Team

Open an issue on the [JASP issue tracker](https://github.com/jasp-stats/jasp-issues/issues) or send a message on [Mattermost](https://mattermost.jasp-stats.org). The JASP team will add it for you.

### Option 2: Pull Request

1. Fork the [`modules-registry`](https://github.com/jasp-stats-modules/modules-registry) repository.
2. Add a new `.yaml` file in the `modules-metadata/` folder. The filename should match your module name (e.g., `jaspMyModule.yaml`). The file needs two fields:
   ```yaml
   name: "jaspMyModule"
   gitUrl: "https://github.com/yourname/jaspMyModule"
   ```
3. Create a pull request to the `modules-registry` repository.
4. After review and a successful merge, the JASP organization will fork your module repository and it will appear in the community modules list. Users running the JASP beta or nightly can then install it.

## Updating a Community Module

Once your module is in the registry, the JASP organization maintains a fork of your repository at `https://github.com/jasp-stats-modules/YourModuleName`. When you have new features or bug fixes ready, open a **cross-fork pull request** from your repository to this fork:

1. **Find the fork**: `https://github.com/jasp-stats-modules/YourModuleName`
2. **Create cross-fork PR**:
   - Go to the JASP fork → Pull Requests → New Pull Request
   - Click **"compare across forks"**
   - Set:
     - Base repository: `jasp-stats-modules/YourModuleName` (JASP's fork)
     - Base branch: `master` or `main`
     - Head repository: `yourname/YourModuleName` (your repo)
     - Compare branch: your update branch
   - Click **Create pull request**
3. **Review**: the JASP team reviews and may request adjustments.
4. **Merge & sync**: once merged, automated systems build and publish a beta release.

## Updating an Official Module

Official modules (`jasp-stats/jasp*`) sync automatically:

- Every push to the module repository triggers a beta bundle build.
- For a proper release, ask a core programmer.
- Update the `NEWS` file with a summary of changes before requesting a release.

## Module Quality Checklists

### Official JASP Module Checklist {#sec-checklist-official}

::: {.callout-important}
All items should be completed before requesting an official release.
:::

- [ ] **Unit tests** with ≥ 70% analysis coverage
- [ ] Auto-generated tests from `.jasp` example files (`makeTestsFromExamples()`)
- [ ] All tests pass in GitHub Actions CI (@sec-ci)
- [ ] Complete **help files** for every analysis (`inst/help/`)
- [ ] `info` properties on all QML elements for inline documentation
- [ ] Informative **README** with module description and screenshots
- [ ] **NEWS / changelog** file maintained and updated before each release
- [ ] Option names follow naming conventions (@sec-option-names)
- [ ] **Upgrades.qml** entries for any renamed/removed options (@sec-upgrades)
- [ ] All user-visible strings wrapped for translation (@sec-i18n)
- [ ] Correct **license headers** on all source files (@sec-licensing)
- [ ] **R style** compliance (@sec-r-style)
- [ ] **QML style** compliance (@sec-qml-style)
- [ ] Standardised **error messages** (@sec-error-checking)

### Community JASP Module Checklist {#sec-checklist-community}

::: {.callout-note}
These are the minimum requirements for a community module to be accepted into the registry.
:::

- [ ] At least **one test per analysis** that runs without error
- [ ] **README** with module description and usage instructions
- [ ] Working example `.jasp` files (in `tests/testthat/jaspfiles/` or `examples/`)
- [ ] Module **installs and loads** without errors in current JASP nightly
- [ ] Valid `DESCRIPTION` file with correct metadata (title, author, version, dependencies)
- [ ] Valid `NAMESPACE` file (auto-generated or correct exports)
- [ ] `Description.qml` with proper module name, description, version, and analysis entries

### Optional but Recommended (Community)

- [ ] Help files or `info` properties on QML elements
- [ ] Translation-ready strings
- [ ] GitHub Actions CI workflow
- [ ] Upgrades.qml for backward compatibility
